text: Does the coupled Burgers-swept-KdV system decompose a Gaussian wave packet (amp=4, sigma=1.5) into a train of >=2 well-separated solitons by T=6 with mass(v) drift < 8%?

bank_use_rationale: no knowledge bank available (NoKB). Following progressive-complexity discipline: E1 is the simplest meaningful baseline. Spectral derivatives are necessary because central FD cannot resolve v_xxx at Nx=256 with the required accuracy for KdV-type behavior; pseudospectral with periodic BC is the textbook baseline. NO dealiasing, NO IMEX, NO splitting — explicit RK4 alone. dt=1e-4 from linear-CFL estimate dt < 2.83/k_max^3 with k_max ~ 26.8.

diagnostics.early_evolution: soliton-train formation starting normally (max v drops 4 -> 1.8 by t=0.36), u grows from 0 to ~3 then suddenly to 7.47 at t=0.38 just before NaN
diagnostics.hypothesized_mode: aliasing instability of quadratic nonlinearities (u*u_x, v^2, u*v) on Fourier basis without 2/3 dealiasing; high-k modes amplified by aliasing then dispersive RK4 advection blows up u

rationale: Code numerically blew up at t=0.388 << T=6. Output is mostly NaN. Phenomenon target not met.

Aliasing-driven blow-up of quadratic nonlinearities is the textbook fix-with-2/3-dealiasing failure mode. Single-component upgrade for E2: add 2/3-rule dealiasing to the pseudospectral derivatives of the nonlinear products. Keep RK4 and dt=1e-4 unchanged.

method: Fourier pseudospectral WITH 2/3-rule dealiasing + explicit RK4
bank_use_rationale: no bank. Single-component upgrade from E1: add 2/3-rule dealiasing to suppress aliasing instability of quadratic nonlinear products (u*u_x, v^2, u*v) observed in F1 (max|u| exploded from ~3 to 7.5 in 200 steps). RK4 and dt=1e-4 unchanged.

rationale: Simulation completes to T=6.0 without blow-up. Final v has 11 distinct peaks with v>=0.8 (target: >=2). Mass drift is 0% to machine precision (target: <8%). Initial Gaussian (max v=4.0) decomposed into multi-peak soliton-like train, consistent with KdV-type inverse-scattering decomposition. Single-component upgrade (2/3 dealiasing) was sufficient — no need for E3.
F2 self-assessed useful and phenomenon target clearly met. Mass drift 0%, >>2 well-separated peaks above 0.8 amplitude. Stop early at iter 2 without consuming iter 3.
